title: FEMS injection scoring + graceful degradation under CPU/GPU load (folded WP-1-FEMS-Injection-Scoring-Graceful-Degradation)
goal: Implement the deterministic scoring formula for MemoryPack item ranking and a progressive retrieval pipeline that degrades gracefully under CPU/GPU load instead of failing hard latency gates. Folded from WP-1-FEMS-Injection-Scoring-Graceful-Degradation. Ports the injection scoring formula from repo governance and the progressive retrieval pattern from memory systems research.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/scoring.rs (InjectionScoringFormula + ScoreInputs types) + src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/progressive_retrieval.rs (ProgressiveRetriever + DegradationTier + DegradationReport types). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/injection_scoring_tests.rs + progressive_retrieval_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-141 (MemoryCapsule), MT-142 (TaskType + DegradationTier enum already defined there), MT-143 (CapsuleBuilder consumes scoring).
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-FEMS-Injection-Scoring-Graceful-Degradation preserved_intent: 'FEMS spec says "drop lowest priority items" on budget overflow but defines no scoring algorithm. The p95 500ms latency target is unrealistic under heavy load'. InjectionScoringFormula: deterministic combination of (a) importance (operator-set weight), (b) recency (exponential decay over recorded_at age), (c) trust (per-source provenance score), (d) outcome-tuned weight (from MT-158), (e) embedding-similarity to query. Formula version exposed as constant `INJECTION_SCORING_FORMULA_VERSION` consumed by RetrievalPolicy.scoring_formula_version (MT-141). ProgressiveRetriever: `pub fn retrieve_progressive(query: &str, top_k: u32, tier: DegradationTier, load_signal: &dyn LoadSignal) -> Result<(Vec<RetrievedItem>, DegradationReport), RetrievalError>`. DegradationTier already defined in MT-142 as `{ Strict, Tiered, Aggressive }`. Tiered pipeline: full-text search -> vector search -> graph expansion -> re-rank; under load_signal pressure, skip latest tiers. DegradationReport: `{ tiers_completed: Vec<String>, tiers_skipped: Vec<String>, load_signal_at_start, total_duration_ms }`. LoadSignal trait: `pub trait LoadSignal { fn current_pressure() -> f64 (0.0..=1.0); }` so production wires to actual CPU/GPU metrics and tests inject deterministic values. Tests: scoring formula is deterministic for fixed inputs; formula version constant stable; progressive retriever completes all tiers under low load; skips graph + re-rank under high load; DegradationReport accurately tracks skipped tiers; never returns empty result when at least one tier succeeded.
risk_if_missed: Without graceful degradation FEMS fails hard under heavy load (parallel local models + browser + other apps), breaking the bounded-injection invariant.
validator_focus: Scoring formula is deterministic; LoadSignal trait is testable; DegradationReport accurately surfaces what was skipped.

implementation_record.summary: MT-161 InjectionScoringFormula (src/memory/scoring.rs, 165 lines, prior session, 4 inline tests) + ProgressiveRetriever (src/memory/progressive_retrieval.rs, 298 lines, prior session, 5 inline tests) verified + integration test files tests/injection_scoring_tests.rs (11 tests) + tests/progressive_retrieval_tests.rs (8 tests) added. Both test files satisfy the contract owned_files entries.

spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_1_no_deferred_live_escape: PASS — scoring.rs is complete (linear combination + recency decay); progressive_retrieval.rs runs all four tier-policy branches without placeholders.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_2_external_resource_touch: PASS — integration tests exercise the public API with deterministic LoadSignal + RetrievalTier injection; scoring tests sweep a 5x6x3x3x3 = 810-input grid.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_3_implementer_cannot_self_certi: PASS — implementations prior session; tests this session; both differ from validator.

red_team_coverage.scoring_formula_deterministic_version_pi: covered — mt161_score_is_deterministic_across_invocations runs 64 invocations + mt161_formula_version_constant_is_pinned + InjectionScoringFormula::version test.
red_team_coverage.load_signal_trait_so_tests_inject_determ: covered — FixedLoad test impl injects deterministic pressure; LoadSignal trait surface is independent of implementation. Production wiring (CPU/GPU pressure) is the responsibility of the consumer.
red_team_coverage.degradation_report_surfaces_every_skippe: covered — mt161_completed_and_skipped_lists_partition_the_four_tiers proves completed + skipped union = {full_text, vector, graph_expansion, re_rank}; mt161_tiered_high_pressure_skips_graph_and_rerank + mt161_aggressive_high_pressure_runs_only_full_text test specific skip patterns.

residual_risks: LoadSignal production wiring (CPU/GPU metrics) is downstream MT scope; integration test uses FixedLoad fake.

FormulaWeights are tunable; a future operator weight change without a formula version bump could silently shift ranking. Validator should check whether 'tune weights without bumping version' is a valid path or requires version bump per minimum_control 'formula deterministic; version constant pinned for reproducibility'.
RetrievalTier trait is injection-based; production must wire BM25 + vector + graph + re-rank backends. Integration test uses fake CountingTier.
No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-161.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Scoring formula is deterministic; version constant pinned for reproducibility.
LoadSignal trait so tests inject deterministic pressure values; production wires real CPU/GPU metrics.
DegradationReport surfaces every skipped tier so the operator can diagnose degraded calls.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: InjectionScoringFormula and ProgressiveRetriever land per contract. Scoring formula is deterministic with a pinned INJECTION_SCORING_FORMULA_VERSION constant. ProgressiveRetriever has DegradationTier-driven tier policy (Strict / Tiered / Aggressive) and a LoadSignal trait for deterministic testing. DegradationReport surfaces completed + skipped tiers. 11 + 8 integration tests pass per builder. CONCERN about permissive-fallback semantics: when an upstream tier fails (e.g., full_text errors), the pipeline still attempts later tiers and returns success if any tier completed. Per the operator stance 'injection scoring permissive-fallback = HIGH', this is the documented design but could mask backend failures from the calibration dashboard. The trade-off is intentional per contract ('never returns empty result when at least one tier succeeded') but the failure mode should be made observable via a distinct status, not buried in skipped[].
